panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth and Jane sit opposite each other in a elegantly furnished drawing-room, bathed in soft morning light. Elizabeth has a thoughtful, concerned expression as she listens, while Jane looks serene and hopeful, her hands gently clasped in her lap. The room features delicate wallpaper and refined wooden furniture.

bubbles.text: "We must endeavour to forget all that has passed on either side," said Jane: "I hope and trust they will yet be happy."

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth, in a plain but graceful Regency-era day dress, stands at a heavy, dark wooden writing-table. Her hand gently rests on a sealed letter, indicating she has just picked it up. Her expression is resolved yet pensive. A quill and ink bottle are neatly arranged on the table, with soft afternoon light illuminating the scene.

Medium shot: Elizabeth, holding the letter, approaches Mrs. Bennet, who is seated on a ornate upholstered armchair. Mrs. Bennet looks up, her face a mix of curiosity and slight impatience. Mary and Kitty are partially visible in the background, subtly observing the interaction. The parlour is filled with decorative items and warm, inviting light.

bubbles.text: "May we take my uncle's letter to read to her?"

panels.description: Close-up: Mrs. Bennet's face, flushed with overwhelming joy and relief, is caught mid-exclamation. Her eyes are wide with delight, and a broad, triumphant smile stretches across her face. Her hands are clasped tightly against her chest in excitement. The background is blurred, emphasizing her intense emotional reaction, with bright, celebratory lighting.

bubbles.text: "My dear, dear Lydia!" cried Mrs. Bennet, "this is delightful indeed! She will be married! I shall see her again! She will be married at sixteen!"

panels.description: Medium shot: Mrs. Bennet, still glowing with excessive joy, gestures emphatically with one hand, her face beaming. Elizabeth stands a little apart, offering a gentle, almost weary smile, attempting to appear calm amidst her mother's effervescence. The parlour setting continues, bathed in an air of triumphant celebration. Sunlight spills into the room.

bubbles.text: "Well," cried her mother, "it is all very right; who should do it but her own uncle?"

panels.description: Medium shot: Mrs. Bennet, full of renewed energy, leans forward in her chair, dictating with grand gestures. Jane sits diligently at a writing desk nearby, a soft, understanding smile on her face as she pens her mother's words. The room feels vibrant with Mrs. Bennet's anticipation and joy. Bright, domestic lighting.

bubbles.text: "In a short time, I shall have a daughter married."
"My dear Jane, I am in such a flutter, that I can't write; so I will dictate, and you write for me."
